notes:
À nha is a playful little tag you add at the end of a sentence to tease someone. It shows “I caught you” energy — joking, suspicious, or mock-threatening.

It’s a sentence-final particle, not a standalone word. You use it after a full statement to add teasing pressure or playful accusation.

Tone is light and cheeky, not serious anger. Similar feeling to English “aha!”, “gotcha!”, or “I see what you did there.”
exampleSentencesTranslated: You just ate all my cake, playful teasing.
I know your secret now, playful teasing.
I saw you texting someone last night, playful teasing.
notes_md:
**À nha** is a playful little tag you add at the end of a sentence to tease someone. It shows “I caught you” energy — joking, suspicious, or mock-threatening.

It’s a sentence-final particle, not a standalone word. You use it after a full statement to add teasing pressure or playful accusation.


Tone is light and cheeky, not serious anger. Similar feeling to English “aha!”, “gotcha!”, or “I see what you did there.”
exampleSentences: Anh vừa ăn hết bánh của em rồi, à nha.
Chị biết bí mật của em rồi, à nha.
Em thấy anh nhắn tin cho ai đó tối qua, à nha.
